instruction: Classify the churn reason expressed in the customer's cancellation message.
baseline_prompt: You are a customer-retention analyst. Classify the churn reason expressed in the customer's cancellation message. Classify into one of these categories: price, coverage, service, speed, moving, competitor.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: Your service costs twice as much as what my neighbor pays with another company for essentially the same features. That doesn't make financial sense to me.
There are too many dead spots along my daily commute and in areas where I spend most of my time. I've tested it extensively and the connectivity is just not there when and where I need it.

Your customer care team promised to escalate my ongoing issue but nothing has changed in over a month. Nobody takes ownership of problems, and I'm constantly being told to call back or wait longer. The lack of proactive communication and problem-solving is why I'm leaving.
Buffering has become a daily nightmare in our household. We can't watch anything in HD, and even basic web browsing feels like we're back in the dial-up era. I need reliable, fast connectivity.
I'm being transferred internationally by my company and will be living abroad starting next quarter, so I'll need to discontinue my account.
I got a mailer from Frontier with a promotional rate that's significantly better than my current bill. I called them and they confirmed the pricing, so I'm going to cancel and switch.
With inflation hitting everything else, I have to make some tough decisions. Cutting this expense will free up money I desperately need for other bills.
I can't get any bars inside most buildings I visit regularly, including my own house. The moment I step indoors, my phone becomes essentially useless. I need a network that actually penetrates walls.

After being promised a callback from management three separate times, no one ever reached out to me. The complete lack of follow-through and accountability in your organization is astounding. I can't trust anything your representatives tell me anymore.

My internet crawls at a snail's pace regardless of the time of day. I've reset the modem dozens of times, but nothing helps. I can't continue with such poor performance when competitors are offering much faster connections in my area.
Hello, I need to terminate my service please. I've accepted a position in another region and will be transferring there by the 15th of next month.
My coworker uses Optimum and pays half of what I do for similar service. I looked into it and they have availability at my address, so I'm making the change.
My company stopped subsidizing my subscription, so now I'm paying the full amount out of pocket. It's just too expensive for me to continue on my own.

I'm canceling because I literally cannot use my phone in half the places I go regularly. Dead zones everywhere - my apartment, the grocery store, even parts of downtown. What's the point of paying for service I can't actually use?

The chat support is useless - I get generic copy-paste responses that don't address my specific situation. When I ask detailed questions, they either ignore them or close the chat. It's like talking to a bot that doesn't actually care about solving problems.

test.query: I've been experiencing extremely sluggish connection rates for the past three months, and it's affecting my ability to work from home. Downloads that should take minutes are taking hours, and video calls constantly buffer. I need to switch to a provider that can deliver better performance.
My lease is ending and I've found an apartment in a different metropolitan area where I'll be starting fresh. Please process my cancellation.
CenturyLink contacted me about their fiber service in my area and the upload speeds are much better for my work-from-home needs. I've already placed an order with them.
I signed up during a promotional period and didn't realize how much the regular rate would be. Now that the discount expired, it's way more than I'm comfortable spending each month.

The signal quality in my area is absolutely terrible. I can't get any reception inside my office building, and even outdoors it's spotty at best. This has been going on since I signed up three months ago.

Every time I contact your company, I get different information from different representatives. One person tells me one thing, another contradicts it completely. The lack of consistency and knowledge among your staff makes it impossible to get accurate help with even simple questions.

Every evening the connection becomes so congested that we can't do anything online. Streaming freezes, downloads stall, and even basic browsing becomes frustrating. The network clearly can't handle the load in our area.
Hi, I need to cancel my subscription as I'm relocating to another state next month and won't be able to continue using your services at my new address.
AT&T reached out with an offer I couldn't refuse - they're matching my current plan but throwing in HBO Max and a tablet for free. I've already signed up with them.
The monthly fee keeps creeping up year after year. I've reached my limit and need to find something more economical that fits my budget better.

My calls keep dropping mid-conversation, and I frequently can't connect at all. The reception in my home is so weak that I have to stand by the window just to make a call. This is completely unacceptable.

The technician who came to my house was unprofessional, left a mess, and didn't fix the problem. When I called to complain and request someone else, I was told I'd have to wait another two weeks. Your field staff needs better training and accountability.

My download rates are nowhere near what was promised when I signed up. I'm getting less than 10% of the advertised bandwidth, and your technicians haven't been able to fix it after multiple visits. I'm done waiting.
We're downsizing and relocating closer to our daughter's family across the country. Please cancel our account as we'll be leaving the area entirely.
After seeing Charter's new customer promotion with no contract and a $200 gift card, I decided it makes more financial sense to switch. My account with them is already active.
Your recent rate increase was the final straw. I'm already stretching my paycheck as it is, and an extra $20 monthly just isn't feasible for my family right now.

The reception problems are making it impossible to work remotely. Video calls are out of the question, and even basic phone calls fail constantly because the signal strength is so poor throughout my entire neighborhood.

I've been trying to resolve a billing error for three weeks now and keep getting transferred between departments with no resolution. Nobody follows up when they say they will, and I'm tired of wasting hours on hold only to repeat my issue to yet another representative who can't help.

The bandwidth we're receiving is completely inadequate for a modern household. With multiple devices connected, everything grinds to a halt. We need faster, more reliable data transmission capabilities.
Due to my military deployment orders, I'm being stationed overseas for the next three years and need to cancel all my current services here.
My neighbor recommended Spectrum and showed me what they're paying versus what I'm paying you. They have faster speeds for less money, so I'm switching over to them this Friday.

I've been with you for three years, but I just can't justify paying this much anymore. My budget has gotten tighter and I need to cut expenses wherever I can. Unfortunately, your service is one of the things I have to let go.
After six months of dealing with weak signals and interrupted service in my daily locations, I've had enough. My previous carrier never had these connectivity issues in the same places.

I had an emergency situation that required immediate assistance, but your support staff showed zero urgency or empathy. They stuck to their script and refused to expedite anything despite my circumstances. When a company can't be flexible in critical moments, it's time to find one that values its customers.
